channel.title: New local problems on FixMyStreet
channel.description: The latest local problems reported by users

item.title: Traffic island needs yellow lines, 9th May
item.description:
The car in my pictures is parked legally, there are no parking restrictions. Cars park in this spot all the time. But. There is little room for buses or large hgv’s to get through if there are cars parked here. I would say I have to help a bus through this space once or twice a week. This evening a 54 bus put its very loud alarm on at 11pm as it couldn’t get through . Annoying! I’d like to request yellow lines outside 173 Croydon Road (my neighbour) by the traffic island. It’s also a pedestrian crossing. This situation has got a lot worse now that ringo has come into force at the Elmer’s end parade of shops!

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 177, Croydon Road, Elmers End, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Recently removed but now fresh grafitti, 9th May
item.description:
A couple of weeks ago this area was cleaned. The lamppost and utility boxes were all nice and clean and some cleaning was done to the walls of the overpass. That same Zoose (Loose?) person spitefully returned and sprayed everywhere again. There's even a bench in the adjacent parking area that they tagged with silver paint. I'm fed up of this character and they must be dealt with. It is disgusting to have our neighbourhood vandalised like this.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Felixstowe Road, West Heath, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Rubbish dumping, 9th May
item.description:
Why not put another recycling bin there ongoing problem

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Perry Street, Crayford, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Street lights not working, 9th May
item.description:
Two street lights opposite each other making it very dark
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 49, Melbourne Grove, East Dulwich, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Abandoned vehicle on foriegn plates, 9th May
item.description:
Silver ford focus on foriegn plates been abandoned. Hasn't moved for ages and no one know who owns it
Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Bullsmoor Lane, Enfield
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Persistent Fly-Tipping and Evidence of Offenders, 9th May
item.description:
What are you doing to catch the individuals responsible for fly-tipping at this specific location? There is an accumulation of waste occurring here every single day. I have found evidence of names/addresses. Please investigate and take action.

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Northwold Road, Hackney, London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Very dangerous broken metal pole, 9th May
item.description:
Sticking out and very dangerous by Grovelands Park entrance

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Broad Walk, Southgate
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Graffiti, 9th May
item.description:
Bravo un Grove Remove graffiti from cable box

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Brabourn Grove, Nunhead, Greater London

item.title: Dumped kitchen parts, 9th May
item.description:
As above

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Leroy Street, Walworth, Greater London

item.title: Builders bag full... Dumped on pavement, 9th May
item.description:
Builders bag full of rubbish ... curtain material and other crud...been there 2 days. Dumped on pedestrian walkway.

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Middle Park Avenue, Eltham
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Abandoned barrier, 9th May
item.description:
This issue was updated to 'fixed' two days ago without it being removed. Please actually remove.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Estate Road, East Dulwich, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Bins over flowing and bins without lids, 9th May
item.description:
You keep making excuses for this house saying that they have no place to store their bins on their property and therefore they can block the pavement? You also keep making excuses for the fact that, a few of their bins do not have lids so THEIR rubbish is slung all over the pavement and road. How is it acceptable that they can block the pavement whilst fly tipping at the same time?

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Arcus Road, Downham, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Unnecessary restricted access to public space, 9th May
item.description:
The temporary metal fencing shown in the photo has been in place since the house (2 Orchard Road, BR1 2PS) shown to the left of the photo was set on fire in October 2024. The fencing is unfairly obstructing the public use of the public green space which is adjacent to the property’s boundary on the Plaistow Lane side. The fencing is a disheveled eyesore and if its purpose is to secure the site, it is failing abysmally. The metal fencing should not encroach the public green space, it should be providing temporary fencing along the fence line on the property boundary only.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Plaistow Lane, Widmore, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Access to pavement?, 9th May
item.description:
Why have you allowed people to continuously park like this taking up all of the pavement for many years now? Imagine if there is someone with a pram or a wheelchair they now have to go into the road because of your incompetence.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Arcus Road, Downham, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Industrial Pressurised Gas Cylinders scattered along Rossington Street, 9th May
item.description:
These are dangerous and have been dumped here for months. The driver in the white truck won’t collect unless reported here! They have a grey body with a bright green shoulder. There are 4/5 cylinders which are large and a hazardous obstruction.

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Rossington Street, Hackney, London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Graffiti, 9th May
item.description:
Lugard Road Just after service Road to 30-44 . Remove graffiti from cable box

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Lugard Road, Peckham, Greater London

item.title: Graffiti, 9th May
item.description:

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Lugard Road, Peckham, Greater London

item.title: Rubbish every where, 9th May
item.description:
Picked up someone for the second time in 2 months .Each time while waiting I noticed only one rubbish bin in a corner .Cigarette butts all over the place and rubbish ,hasn’t been swept in ages if ever lots of piles of dirts and leaves not a very good impression for a hospital

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Mortimer Road, Northumberland Heath, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Street light leaning over, 9th May
item.description:
Lugard Road outside Evan Cook Close Street light is leaning over. Appears to have been hit

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Lugard Road, Nunhead, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet
